selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 12.8% and 20-day momentum is 27.9%.
Trend quality is supported by 80% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 16.3% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 3.4%, downside volatility near 1.9%, and a recent max drawdown of 3.4%.
Volume confirmation is 55.4% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 13.1% versus SPY over 5 days and 13.0% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.85, confidence 0.78, and 0.8 weighted articles across 4 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.85, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 4 articles across 4 sources, 0.8 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.75, average source quality 0.81, confidence 0.78.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Price action and news are pointing in the same direction, which raises trust in the setup.
Overall model score is 0.289 with high confidence.

news_evidence.title: Cisco Systems Inc. filed 10-Q: 10-Q
news_evidence.llm_reason: Official Cisco 10-Q filing for the quarter is directly company-specific and potentially market-moving in the short term depending on disclosure of results, guidance, or material items. It is the most relevant item for 1–10 day horizon.

news_evidence.title: Palo Alto Networks Up 70%: Can the Rally Last Into June?
news_evidence.llm_reason: Analysis of Palo Alto Networks’ rally is sector-relevant (cybersecurity) and could influence sentiment toward networking/security peers like Cisco, but it is focused on a different company and thus has limited direct short-term impact on CSCO.
